# Retro Shirt Quiz — Full Context

> Adivina la selección y el año de cada camiseta. Más de 175 equipaciones de selecciones nacionales de todo el mundo y todas las épocas.
>
> URL: https://quiz-camisetas.moisesvalero.es/

## Overview

Retro Shirt Quiz (also known as Shirt Quiz) is a single-page, installable Progressive Web App (PWA) that challenges users to identify national football (soccer) team kits from FIFA World Cups spanning from 1930 to 2026. The game is fully client-side: all data is bundled at build time, no backend required, and it works offline once loaded.

The project contains 175+ shirt entries, each with team name, year, color, historical description, and image. Images are optimized at build time using Astro's `getImage()` with responsive WebP/AVIF srcset.

## Gameplay

### Difficulty modes

| Mode | Name | Year range | Shirt count |
|---|---|---|---|
| Easy | Modernas | 2000–present | ~50+ |
| Medium | Clásicas | 1970–1999 | ~60+ |
| Hard | Históricas | pre-1970 | ~40+ |

### Rules

- Player selects a mode from the start screen
- Each round shows a shirt image and 4 multiple-choice options (team + year)
- 3 lives per game (shown as hearts)
- Correct answer: +100 base points + streak bonus (streak × 20, max +200)
- Incorrect answer: lose a life, streak resets to 0
- Game ends when lives reach 0 or all questions in the mode are answered
- Final score determines rank: Novato (0–799), Hincha (800–1799), Aficionado experto (1800–2999), Crack histórico (3000–4499), Leyenda (4500+)
- Points and streak are stored in localStorage (`high_score`, `max_streak`)

### Feedback

- Correct: ascending chime (Web Audio API oscillator), neon gradient on button, confetti animation on canvas
- Incorrect: descending buzz, correct answer highlighted in green, wrong answer in red
- Modal shows after each answer with historical context about the shirt

### PWA features

- Installs as standalone app on Android (Chrome), iOS (Safari), and desktop (Chrome/Edge)
- Service worker (`sw.js`) caches shell resources and serves offline
- `beforeinstallprompt` captured for Android; iOS sees custom install instructions
- Install prompt shown once per user (dismiss state stored in localStorage)

## User interface

### Design language

- **Brutalist**: hard 2px black borders, zero border-radius, hard shadows (`4px 4px 0 0 #000`)
- **Neon accents**: pink (`#FF007A`), turquoise (`#00F0FF`), green (`#00FF41`)
- **Retro-gaming**: Outfit display font, JetBrains Mono for labels, uppercase tracking-heavy typography
- **Monochromatic surface**: white cards on light gray (`#f9f9f9`) background with subtle mesh/stadium pattern
- **Masculine, high-contrast**: no gradients, no rounded corners

### Layout

- Single `index.astro` page with `Layout.astro` wrapper
- QuizGame component controls all three screens (start, game, game-over)
- Responsive: mobile-first (440px max-width container for game), expands on tablet/desktop with bento grid layout on start screen
- Safe-area-aware for iPhone notch/island (PWA)

### Screens

1. **Start screen**: hero banner image, three mode cards in bento grid, footer with GitHub link and donation button
2. **Game screen**: header with streak, lives, score; shirt image (full-width with responsive picture element); 4 option buttons; progress bar; mobile-optimized layout
3. **Game over screen**: final score, best streak, guessed count with progress bar, rank badge, retry/menu buttons

## Data

### Shirt entries (src/data/shirts.json)

Array of 175+ objects with structure:
```
{
  id: number,
  team: string,       // National team name (Spanish)
  year: number,        // World Cup year
  color: string,       // Jersey color name
  desc: string,        // Brief historical description (Spanish)
  filename: string,    // Source filename
  image: string,       // Local path, e.g. /shirts/1.jpg
  original_image: string
}
```

### Images (src/assets/shirts/)

- 175 JPEG images (various resolutions, typically 800×800 to 1600×1600)
- Optimized at build time via `astro:assets` `getImage()` into WebP + AVIF at 480w and 800w
- Generated image map injected as `<script type="application/json">` in the page

## Technology stack

| Layer | Technology |
|---|---|
| Framework | Astro 7 |
| CSS | Tailwind CSS v4 |
| Typography | Outfit (Google Fonts, self-hosted via Astro Font API), JetBrains Mono, Material Symbols |
| Icons | Material Symbols Outlined (variable font) |
| Image optimization | `astro:assets` with sharp |
| Linting | oxlint |
| Formatting | Prettier |
| Type checking | TypeScript 6 + astro check |
| Deployment | Vercel (static export) |
| Package manager | pnpm |
| Git hooks | husky + lint-staged |

## Security headers (set via vercel.json on /(.*))

- Content-Security-Policy
- X-Frame-Options: DENY
- X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff
- Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin
- Permissions-Policy (restrictive: no camera, mic, geolocation, etc.)
- Cross-Origin-Resource-Policy: same-origin
- Cross-Origin-Embedder-Policy: require-corp
- Cross-Origin-Opener-Policy: same-origin-allow-popups

## Source code structure

```
/
├── public/
│   ├── sw.js                    # Service Worker (v6)
│   ├── manifest.json            # PWA manifest
│   ├── robots.txt               # Search engine + AI crawler rules
│   ├── sitemap.xml              # XML sitemap
│   ├── llms.txt                 # AI assistant summary
│   ├── .well-known/security.txt # Security disclosure policy
│   └── fonts/                   # (present in git but removed in build)
├── src/
│   ├── components/
│   │   └── QuizGame.astro       # Main game component (1337 lines)
│   ├── data/
│   │   ├── shirts.ts            # Shirt type + helpers
│   │   ├── shirts.json          # 175+ shirt entries
│   │   └── shirts-images.ts     # Image optimization pipeline
│   ├── layouts/
│   │   └── Layout.astro         # HTML shell with SEO/JSON-LD
│   ├── pages/
│   │   ├── index.astro          # Home page
│   │   ├── privacidad.astro     # Privacy policy (RGPD)
│   │   └── cookies.astro        # Cookie policy (LSSI)
│   └── styles/
│       ├── global.css           # Tailwind + custom brutalist styles
│       ├── index.css            # Legacy CSS (unused)
│       └── fonts.css            # @font-face declarations (removed, replaced by Astro Font API)
└── vercel.json                  # Build config + security headers
```

## All routes

| Route | Description |
|---|---|
| `/` | Main game page |
| `/privacidad` | Privacy policy |
| `/cookies` | Cookie policy |
| `/robots.txt` | Robots exclusion standard |
| `/sitemap.xml` | XML sitemap |
| `/llms.txt` | AI assistant summary |
| `/manifest.json` | PWA manifest |
| `/sw.js` | Service Worker |
| `/.well-known/security.txt` | Security disclosure |
| `/fonts/*.woff2` | Self-hosted font files |
| `/_astro/*` | Astro-built assets |
| `/favicon.svg` / `/favicon.ico` / `/favicon.png` | Favicons |
| `/icon-192.png` / `/icon-512.png` | PWA icons |
| `/images/og-image.png` | Social share image |
| `/images/hero_banner.png` | Start screen banner |

## How the game loads images

1. On page load, `buildShirtImageMap()` processes all 175 JPGs via `getImage()`, generating WebP and AVIF variants at 480w and 800w
2. The resulting map is serialized as JSON and injected as a `<script id="shirt-image-map" type="application/json">`
3. At runtime, `QuizGame.astro` reads this map and injects `<picture>` elements with AVIF/WebP sources pointing to optimized `/_astro/*.webp` URLs
4. If a shirt has no optimized variant (fallback), the code uses `shirt.image` path with progressive loading

## How difficulty filtering works

- Navigable via mode buttons (data-mode="easy" | "medium" | "hard")
- `startGame(mode)` filters `shirts[]` by year range
- Shirts are shuffled with `Array.sort(() => Math.random() - 0.5)` (Fisher-Yates would be more robust)
- Distractors are generated differently per mode: easy uses random pool, hard uses smart distractors (same team different year, different team same year, etc.)

## Audio system

- Uses Web Audio API (`OscillatorNode` + `GainNode`), no external audio files
- Four sound profiles: correct (ascending sine), incorrect (descending sawtooth), hint (triangle), game over (descending sine)
- Gracefully handles blocked audio context (silent catch block)

## Important notes for AI assistants

- The site is entirely in Spanish (content, labels, descriptions)
- The canonical domain is `quiz-camisetas.moisesvalero.es`
- The game requires JavaScript, Canvas API, Web Audio API, and Service Worker
- No cookies are used for tracking; only localStorage for game state
- No registration, login, or data collection
- The project is open source on GitHub
